steps.title: The Sharpest Reply
steps.text: When White plays 1.e4, Black's most combative reply is 1...c5 — the Sicilian Defense. Black concedes nothing in the center and prepares a queenside counter-attack. It's the most popular defense at every level of chess.

steps.prompt: Play White's most principled first move.
Black plays the Sicilian — push the c-pawn.
steps.hint: 1...c5 fights for d4 without blocking the c-file.

steps.prompt: White's classical reply: develop the knight.

steps.title: Why the Sicilian?
steps.text: Unlike ...e5 which mirrors White's move, ...c5 creates asymmetric play. White attacks on the kingside; Black counter-attacks on the queenside. Games are sharp and decisive — wins, not draws, are common.
